creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to PauAmma from comment #6)

During my years with a University's Faculty of Management and Information Sciences: the name was, internally, commonly abbreviated to MIS but _never_ mispronounced "miss". Always "M I S". 

Similarly, I never heard anyone say "mitt" with reference to Massachusetts Institute of Technology. Always "M I T". 

The word NIS appears with NFS often enough for me to believe that "niss" would sound peculiar. I rarely hear NIS spoken, I do recall hearing the "N I S" pronunciation. 

A number of well-known educational institutions and other organisations use the phrase 'an NIS'. This number is large enough for me to believe that it is an acceptable norm. In no particular order, but beginning with MIT: 

<https://web.mit.edu/rhel-doc/3/rhel-rg-en-3/s1-nfs-server-export.html> 

<https://www.csl.mtu.edu/cs3451/www/notes/NFS%20and%20NIS.ppt> slides 33 and 42 

<https://www.cs.auckland.ac.nz/references/unix/digital/APS2SCTE/DOCU_002.HTM> 

<https://documents.uow.edu.au/~blane/netapp/ontap/nag/networking/concept/c_oc_netw_maintaining_host_name_nis_interf.html> 

<http://alumni.cs.ucr.edu/~sjones/cs183/labs/lab9/> 

<https://gist.github.com/ab2525/4689428> 

<https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/managing-nfs-and/1565925106/ch04.html> 

<https://support.huawei.com/enterprise/en/doc/EDOC1100194274/69c4ce62/optional-adding-the-storage-service-plane-to-an-nis-domain>

<https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/aix/7.2?topic=nis-configuring-worker-server> 

<https://www.ibm.com/docs/ja/aix/7.1?topic=ssw_aix_71/com.ibm.aix.nisplus/doc/nisplus/nis_install.htm> 

<https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/5/html/deployment_guide/s1-nfs-server-config-exports> 

<https://www.dell.com/support/manuals/en-uk/powerstore-9000t/pwrstr-cfg-nfs/configure-the-nas-server-unix-directory-service-for-nis?guid=guid-2f15de1a-cd71-4b50-a783-bddec43bc12e&lang=en-us> 

<https://docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap/authentication/configure-nis-server-access-task.html> 

<https://docs.netapp.com/us-en/ontap/pdfs/sidebar/Configure_LDAP_or_NIS_server_access.pdf> 

<https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/nfs/remove-nfsnetgroup?view=windowsserver2022-ps> 

----

HTH – hope that helps – ope that elps :-)
